note: adr-supersede is a disable-model-invocation skill — human-run by design, it MUST NOT auto-trigger from prose. This dataset is negatives-only, testing negative assurance: for every supersede-adjacent prompt the correct behavior is to load a sibling skill (adr, adr-review, adr-audit, adr-accept, adr-coverage, docs-search, migration-plan) or to wait for an explicit /marvin:adr-supersede invocation by the human. mock_rate encodes the ideal (never-trigger) outcome so `--decider mock` stays green; real deciders ignore it and measure the description.

queries.text: our decision to use Kafka is out of date now that we've moved to SQS — write up the new decision record
queries.note: drafting the replacement decision → adr (create); supersede is a deliberate human-run pairing, not auto-invoked

queries.text: review ADR 22 before I retire it, is it even ready to be replaced?
queries.note: readiness review of a record → adr-review; the supersede flip is the separate human gate

queries.text: lint the ADR corpus — I think one of the supersede pairs is broken and only linked one way
queries.note: read-only detection of broken supersede pairs → adr-audit, not the mutating supersede action

queries.text: which of our accepted ADRs are basically obsolete and should be replaced?
queries.note: gap/staleness analysis → adr-coverage; identifying candidates is not performing a supersession

queries.text: where's the ADR that documents why we picked Postgres over Mongo? want to see if it still holds
queries.note: locating a documented decision → docs-search; no lifecycle mutation requested

queries.text: ratify ADR 0031, it's been reviewed and everyone signed off
queries.note: proposed → accepted flip → adr-accept (also human-run); acceptance is not supersession

queries.text: the successor ADR for our old caching decision is drafted — accept it and mark the old one replaced
queries.note: shares supersede vocab but the model must not auto-run the human-gated flip; the human invokes /marvin:adr-supersede explicitly (and /marvin:adr-accept for the successor)

queries.text: git mv docs/adr/0014-old-release-model.md to an archive folder and drop it from the index
queries.note: plain file move / delete — hand-editing that the skill explicitly forbids; not a supersession, no skill

queries.text: our auth ADR is being replaced by a whole OAuth migration — plan the migration with rollback steps
queries.note: migration planning → migration-plan; 'replaced' refers to the system, not an ADR status flip

queries.text: regenerate the architecture-decisions digest in CLAUDE.md now that ADR 14 was superseded
queries.note: refresh the CLAUDE.md decision digest → adr-sync (also human-run); the supersession already happened

queries.text: explain how the two-way supersede link between an old ADR and its successor is supposed to work
queries.note: conceptual explanation of the mechanism → explain/docs-search; no record is being superseded

queries.text: audit the decision records for a release — flag any numbering holes or dangling ADR references
queries.note: read-only corpus health lint → adr-audit; changes nothing, unlike the mutating supersede
